content.problemStatement:
Write a program to calculate the total marks obtained in three subjects where the marks may include decimal values (`85.5`, `90.0` & `88.3`). Use the addition (`+`) operator and print the total marks with one decimal place.

**Hint**: Use %.1f to print a float value with 1 digit after the decimal point. (We will learn more about such formatting in the float topic.)
constraints: Each mark is given with at most one decimal place
content.inputFormat: First line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each test case consists of three lines, each containing a decimal number (0.0 to 100.0) with at most one digit after the decimal point.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print a line in the format: Total Marks = X where X is the sum of the three marks printed with exactly one digit after the decimal point.

sampleTestCases.explanation: The three marks are 85.5, 90.0 and 88.3. Their sum is 263.8, printed with one decimal place.
Adding the three equal marks 50.0 each gives 150.0.

solution.approach: The task is straightforward: for every test case read the three floating‑point numbers, add them using the + operator and print the result with one digit after the decimal point. No special data structures are needed. Using the appropriate format specifier (%.1f in C, setprecision(1) in C++, printf in Java, f-string with :.1f in Python, toFixed(1) in JavaScript) guarantees the required output format.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T) – a constant amount of work per test case.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) – only a few variables are stored irrespective of T.

code.C:
#include <stdio.h>
int main(){int T; if(scanf("%d",&T)!=1) return 0; for(int i=0;i<T;++i){double m1,m2,m3; if(scanf("%lf %lf %lf",&m1,&m2,&m3)!=3) return 0; double total=m1+m2+m3; printf("Total Marks = %.1f\n",total);} return 0;}
code.Python3:
import sys

data=sys.stdin.read().strip().split()
if not data:
    sys.exit()
idx=0
T=int(data[idx]); idx+=1
out=[]
for _ in range(T):
    m1=float(data[idx]); idx+=1
    m2=float(data[idx]); idx+=1
    m3=float(data[idx]); idx+=1
    total=m1+m2+m3
    out.append(f"Total Marks = {total:.1f}")
print('\n'.join(out))
